other_observers: Larry Semo, Doug Faulkner, and Richard Stevens; First seen by Abby Grier and Jim & Karen Schmoker, first reported by Bill Schmoker
specific_location: Just west of the Nature Center Parking lot at Barr Lake State Park
description: Bright red bird. The red was on head, breast, belly, flanks, and undertail coverts. It had a black mask going through the eye. Black back and tail. Black legs and a small black bill.Behaviors: It was usually sitting fairly low to half way up the trees. It would occasionally sit on top of the mullens. It would then fly from its perch down close to the ground to catch moths.Call: didn't call or singPlumage: Adult
